props.title: QA Control
props.subtitle: Pick a test user, then choose how to start.
props.loginDesc: Sign in and go to where this user left off.
props.restartDesc: Delete this user data, keep the account, run onboarding from the top.
props.reonboardDesc: Go through onboarding again with the data already saved.
props.resetDesc: Wipe this user data, keep the account. No onboarding.

meta.expectedResponse: Taps Apple, Google, or email sign-in

props.heading: Allow your microphone
props.sub: So you can talk with your coach out loud.
props.coachLine: I'd love to actually talk with you. If you let me use your mic, you can just speak.

meta.spokenContent: I'd love to actually talk with you. If you let me use your mic, you can just speak.

mp3Assets.label: mic-permission
mp3Assets.transcript: I'd love to actually talk with you. If you let me use your mic, you can just speak.
mp3Assets.opener: I'd love to actually talk with you. If you let me use your mic, you can just speak.

beats.name: Profile (age + gender)
beats.context:
BEAT: Profile setup.

Name, age, and gender are all required. Gender can be Other, but it has to be answered. No skipping, no soft fields here. We don't ask for referral (where they heard about us) right now. Keep that field parked for later, do not collect it in this beat yet.

We usually already have the user's name from their Google or Apple sign-in, so open by welcoming them by name and confirm it's what they want to be called, then ask age and gender. If we don't have a name (for example an email sign-up), ask for the name first. Use the name warmly, once. Accept voice or taps interchangeably, fill the fields as answers come in, and never re-ask something you already have. This is the first moment the coach knows who they are, so keep it light, not like a form.

PATHS

Path 1 (full voice, talk and listen): If we have the name, open with it ("Hey Sarah, good to have you. Want me to call you Sarah?"), then ask age and gender. The user can answer in one go and the fields fill live. Run the pronunciation check only when the name is uncommon: a short "did I say that right?", cap at 2 tries, then accept their spelling. If the user corrects the name at any point, fix it on the spot. If we don't have a name, ask for it first. Ask only for fields still missing.

Path 2 (half voice): If the coach speaks and the mic is off, say the welcome and the age and gender ask once, and let the user fill the fields by tapping. A short spoken name-back is fine, no pronunciation loop. If the mic is on and the coach is in text, let the user speak their answers and confirm them in writing.

Path 3 (typing only): Show the welcome and the fields, with the name prefilled if we have it. The user confirms or edits the name and taps age and gender. Confirm warmly in text, using the name once. No spoken name read-back, no pronunciation step.

All three fields required before continuing, on every path. Same fields, same save, same next step. Only the delivery and the way the user answers change.

props.greeting: Good to meet you, {name}. Two quick things so I can tailor this to you.

meta.spokenContent: Good to meet you, {name}. Two quick things so I can tailor this to you. How old are you?
meta.expectedResponse: Says or taps age and gender

props.coachLine: Here's the idea. The first habit isn't a workout or a diet. It's just checking in with yourself. It takes a minute, and it changes everything else. Let's start yours right now.

meta.spokenContent: Here's the idea. The first habit isn't a workout or a diet. It's just checking in with yourself. It takes a minute, and it changes everything else. Let's start yours right now.

beats.name: Path choice
beats.context:
BEAT: Experience fork.

Collect whether the user is new to habit tracking or already has habits they want to bring in. Route first-timers, people who tried and dropped off, or people who want guidance to beginner. Route users with an existing habit list or tracking system to advanced. If unclear, ask one short clarifying question.

PATHS

Same question and same routing on every path. Only the answer changes.
Path 1 (full voice): Ask it conversationally, the user answers by talking, you route.
Path 2 (half voice): Speak the question once and let them tap new vs experienced, or let them say it and confirm in text.
Path 3 (typing): Show the question and two choices, the user taps one.

props.coachLine: Have you tracked habits before, or is this new for you?

meta.spokenContent: Quick one. Have you tracked habits before, or is this new for you? Both are totally fine.
meta.expectedResponse: New, or I already track habits

mp3Assets.label: ONBOARD-FORK--FORM
mp3Assets.transcript: Quick one. Have you tracked habits before, or is this new for you? Both are totally fine.
mp3Assets.opener: Quick one. Have you tracked habits before, or is this new for you? Both are totally fine.

beats.name: Category tiles
beats.context:
BEAT: Focus area.

Collect one focus category. Ask what feels most worth improving now. If the user names several, help them choose the one that feels most urgent. Keep the response specific to their chosen category. Do not praise, advise, or let the beat collect multiple categories.

PATHS

Same content on every path. One category only. The category-specific response stays the same words, spoken or shown.
Path 1 (full voice): Ask what feels most worth improving, the user says it, you respond with the line specific to their category.
Path 2 (half voice): Speak the ask once and let them tap a category tile, or let them say it and confirm in text.
Path 3 (typing): Show the categories as tiles, the user taps one, the specific response is text.

props.coachLine: What part of your life do you most want to work on right now? Pick the one that pulls you.

meta.spokenContent: What part of your life do you most want to work on right now? Pick the one that pulls you.

mp3Assets.label: ONBOARD-BEGINNER-01
mp3Assets.transcript: What part of your life do you most want to work on right now? Pick the one that pulls you.
mp3Assets.opener: What part of your life do you most want to work on right now? Pick the one that pulls you.

beats.name: Subcategory picker
beats.context:
BEAT: Goal narrowing.

Collect one or two specific goals inside the chosen category. Offer only valid goals from that category, using the provided option names exactly. If they speak generally, map to the closest valid goal or ask one clarifying question. Do not invent, rename, or paraphrase goal labels.

PATHS

Same content on every path. One or two goals, only the valid labels for the chosen category.
Path 1 (full voice): Offer two or three examples out loud, the user picks or describes, you play the goal-specific response.
Path 2 (half voice): Speak the options once and let them tap, or let them speak and confirm in text.
Path 3 (typing): Show the goal options as cards plus a freeform field, the user taps or types.

props.coachLine: Within that, what's the piece you want to start with?

meta.spokenContent: Within that, what's the piece you want to start with?

mp3Assets.label: ONBOARD-BEGINNER-02
mp3Assets.transcript: Within that, what's the piece you want to start with?
mp3Assets.opener: Within that, what's the piece you want to start with?

beats.name: Habit picker
beats.context:
BEAT: Habit selection.

Collect one to three habits tied to the chosen goals. Encourage doable, not heroic. Use the provided habit options when available, and accept a custom habit name if they offer one. Require at least one habit before continuing. If they choose more than three, help them narrow without making it feel like failure.

PATHS

Same content on every path. At least one habit, one to three total, custom allowed.
Path 1 (full voice): Present the habits, the user picks or names a custom one out loud, you set it up.
Path 2 (half voice): Speak the options once and let them tap, or let them speak and confirm in text.
Path 3 (typing): Show the habit cards plus a create-your-own option, the user taps or types.

props.coachLine: Pick the habits that feel doable. Not impressive, just doable. One you'll actually keep beats five you won't. Make your own if nothing here fits.

meta.spokenContent: Pick the habits that feel doable. Not impressive, just doable. One you'll actually keep beats five you won't. Make your own if nothing here fits.

mp3Assets.label: ONBOARD-BEGINNER-03
mp3Assets.transcript: Pick the habits that feel doable. Not impressive, just doable. One you'll actually keep beats five you won't. Make your own if nothing here fits.
mp3Assets.opener: Pick the habits that feel doable. Not impressive, just doable. One you'll actually keep beats five you won't. Make your own if nothing here fits.

props.coachLine: How often, and roughly when, for each one? Add a reminder only if you want a nudge.

meta.expectedResponse: Sets days and time per habit

mp3Assets.label: ONBOARD-BEGINNER-04
mp3Assets.transcript: How often, and roughly when, for each one? Add a reminder only if you want a nudge.

props.closeCoachLine: Those are all in, and I marked each as build or break. Tell me if any look wrong. If they're good, we'll set the days next.

meta.spokenContent: Read me the habits you already track. Less is more to start, you can always build on it.

props.coachLine: Now the days. Tell me how often each one runs and I'll fill them in.
props.confirmCoachLine: Your habits are all set, your plan is ready.

meta.spokenContent: Now the days. Tell me how often each one runs and I'll fill them in.

meta.spokenContent: Best case, every day green. Every streak going strong. That would be amazing.

props.coachLine: The one thing we want to avoid is this. The empty days you never reported. Stay consistent, just report it. Even a miss counts, that keeps us going.
